COMPENDIUM.effect: Disguises Link as a Garo (Garo Master), allowing him to summon and battle the ghostly Garo in Ikana, and is needed to advance in Ikana Canyon (it also makes ReDeads/Gibdos dance).
COMPENDIUM.where: Win the horseback obstacle race against the Gorman Brothers at the Gorman Track on Milk Road (riding Epona); they reward you with the mask.

COMPENDIUM.name: Captain's Hat
COMPENDIUM.effect: Lets Link command the undead Stalchildren of Ikana as their captain (they obey orders and open paths), and makes nearby ReDeads dance helplessly.
COMPENDIUM.where: In the Ikana Graveyard, play the Sonata of Awakening to wake Captain Keeta, chase and defeat him; open the big chest he leaves to claim the Captain's Hat.

COMPENDIUM.name: Gibdo Mask
COMPENDIUM.effect: Disguises Link as a Gibdo so the mummies stop attacking and will talk; it is required to pass the item-demanding Gibdos Beneath the Well in Ikana.
COMPENDIUM.where: Play the Song of Storms to open the Music Box House in Ikana Canyon, go inside to the basement and play the Song of Healing for the grieving man (Pamela's father, cursed into a Gibdo).

COMPENDIUM.name: Giant's Mask
COMPENDIUM.effect: Turns Link into a giant for a limited time, used to grapple and defeat the boss Twinmold in the Stone Tower Temple.
COMPENDIUM.where: In the Stone Tower Temple, found in a large treasure chest that appears after defeating the second Eyegore in the room just BEFORE the Twinmold boss door (original N64 version — the 3DS remake instead places it in a chest that appears mid-fight after killing the blue Twinmold).

COMPENDIUM.name: Ocarina of Time
COMPENDIUM.effect: The magical ocarina used to play songs that warp time, change day/night, slow the three-day clock, and solve countless puzzles. Playing the Song of Time resets the cycle back to the dawn of the First Day.
COMPENDIUM.where: Recovered from the Skull Kid in the Clock Tower interior at midnight on the Final Day; the Happy Mask Salesman then teaches Link the Song of Time.

COMPENDIUM.name: Hookshot
COMPENDIUM.effect: Fires a chain-tipped spike that latches onto wooden posts, boards, trees, and some targets to pull Link across gaps or yank distant items toward him. Also stuns or damages many enemies.
COMPENDIUM.where: Reward for defeating the Wizzrobe mini-boss inside the Pirates' Fortress (Great Bay), encountered while rescuing the Zora eggs.

COMPENDIUM.name: Bombs (Bomb Bag)
COMPENDIUM.effect: Throwable explosives carried in the Bomb Bag; blow open cracked walls, destroy enemies, and trigger switches. The starting Bomb Bag holds 20 bombs.
COMPENDIUM.where: Buy the Bomb Bag at the Bomb Shop in West Clock Town (50 Rupees); bombs themselves refill from shops, grass, and pots.

COMPENDIUM.name: Bombchu
COMPENDIUM.effect: Mobile mouse-like bombs: set one down and it scurries forward along the ground or up walls, then explodes — useful for hitting switches and enemies at a distance or around corners.
COMPENDIUM.where: Purchased from the Bomb Shop in West Clock Town (10 for 40 Rupees), and found in chests/grottos.

COMPENDIUM.name: Powder Keg
COMPENDIUM.effect: A giant barrel of explosives far more powerful than a bomb — clears huge boulders. Only Goron Link can carry one (it slows him), and only one can be held at a time.
COMPENDIUM.where: Earn Medigoron's approval by passing his test in Goron Village as Goron Link (carry a Powder Keg to blast the boulder blocking the Goron Racetrack on the road to Goron Village); afterward buy them from the Goron at the West Clock Town Bomb Shop or from Medigoron.

COMPENDIUM.name: Lens of Truth
COMPENDIUM.effect: Reveals hidden things while held: invisible enemies, hidden platforms, secret passages, false walls, and spirits (e.g., Darmani). Drains magic while in use.
COMPENDIUM.where: Inside a chest in a cave across the chasm in Goron Village — follow the owl Kaepora Gaebora's dropped feathers across the gap to reach it.

COMPENDIUM.name: Magic Bean
COMPENDIUM.effect: Plant a bean in soft soil and water it with spring water to instantly grow a leaf/plant Link can ride up to higher areas.
COMPENDIUM.where: Bought from the wandering Bean Seller in a hole near the Deku Palace gardens / Southern Swamp, 10 Rupees apiece (the price climbs as you buy more).

COMPENDIUM.name: Pictograph Box
COMPENDIUM.effect: A camera that takes pictographs (photos); required for several sidequests, such as photographing the Deku King, Tingle, or the Gerudo pirate captain.
COMPENDIUM.where: Given by Koume at the Swamp Tourist Center after you rescue her in the Woods of Mystery (Southern Swamp).

COMPENDIUM.name: Empty Bottle
COMPENDIUM.effect: Reusable container that holds potions, fairies, water, eggs, bugs, and quest items. Majora's Mask has six bottles total.
COMPENDIUM.where: Six sources: (1) Kotake's free Red Potion bottle for saving Koume; (2) Gold Dust from winning the Goron Race; (3) Romani's bottle of milk for defending the ranch cows from the aliens; (4) Madame Aroma's bottle of Chateau Romani from the Anju & Kafei 'Special Delivery to Mama' quest; (5) winning the beaver brothers' race in Great Bay; (6) helping Dampe dig in Ikana Graveyard (Captain's Hat needed).

COMPENDIUM.name: Red Potion
COMPENDIUM.effect: Drink to fully restore all hearts (health). Also cures the Blue Bubble jinx.
COMPENDIUM.where: Bought from the Magic Hags' Potion Shop, Trading Post, Zora Shop, and Goron Shop; Kotake also gives a free bottle of Red Potion for rescuing Koume.

COMPENDIUM.name: Green Potion
COMPENDIUM.effect: Drink to fully restore the Magic Meter.
COMPENDIUM.where: Bought from the Magic Hags' Potion Shop in the Southern Swamp.

COMPENDIUM.name: Blue Potion
COMPENDIUM.effect: Drink to fully restore both hearts (health) and the Magic Meter; also removes a Blue Bubble's jinx.
COMPENDIUM.where: Bought from the Ikana Canyon Business Scrub (100 Rupees), or have Kotake brew them (60 Rupees each) after giving her a Magic Mushroom.

COMPENDIUM.name: Fairy
COMPENDIUM.effect: A bottled fairy heals several hearts when used, and automatically revives Link with health if he falls in battle while carrying one.
COMPENDIUM.where: Catch a stray/wild fairy in a bottle (e.g., from Great Fairy Fountains or fairies floating in the field), or scoop one up after it appears.

COMPENDIUM.name: Chateau Romani
COMPENDIUM.effect: Premium Romani Ranch milk that restores hearts and grants unlimited (infinite) magic for the rest of the current three-day cycle.
COMPENDIUM.where: Given by Madame Aroma (via the Anju & Kafei 'Special Delivery to Mama' quest), or buy it at the Milk Bar 'Latte' in East Clock Town (200 Rupees) — entry requires Romani's Mask.

COMPENDIUM.name: Hot Spring Water
COMPENDIUM.effect: Bottled hot water that melts ice — thaws the frozen Goron Elder and frozen blocks in the Mountain Village/Goron areas. Cools to ordinary spring water after a short time.
COMPENDIUM.where: Scoop it from the hot spring in the cave where you got the Goron Mask (Darmani's resting place) or from the hot-spring hole on Twin Islands near Goron Village.

COMPENDIUM.name: Zora Egg
COMPENDIUM.effect: A Zora egg carried in a bottle; used to deliver the rescued eggs to the Marine Research Lab so the scientist can teach Lulu's song (New Wave Bossa Nova). Has no other functional use.
COMPENDIUM.where: Collected from the Pirates' Fortress and Pinnacle Rock in Great Bay (seven eggs total) as Zora Link, scooped into bottles.

COMPENDIUM.name: Gold Dust
COMPENDIUM.effect: Rare reagent given to Zubora and Gabora at the Mountain Smithy to forge the Gilded Sword — the permanent, stronger upgrade of the Razor Sword.
COMPENDIUM.where: Won by placing first in the Goron Race (Goron Racetrack) for the first time, as Goron Link; comes in its own new bottle.

COMPENDIUM.name: Bombers' Notebook
COMPENDIUM.effect: A scheduling notebook that tracks the comings, goings, and personal events of Clock Town's people across the three days, logging sidequests and the rewards (often Heart Pieces and masks) you can earn by helping them.
COMPENDIUM.where: Join the Bombers' Secret Society of Justice — play hide-and-seek and catch all five Bomber kids around Clock Town before dawn; their leader Jim then gives you the Notebook and the secret-hideout code.

COMPENDIUM.name: Magic Spin Attack (Spin Attack upgrade)
COMPENDIUM.effect: Charging the sword unleashes a spinning slash that hits all around Link. With magic, holding the charge produces a stronger magic-powered Spin Attack (the blue spin).
COMPENDIUM.where: Granted by the Great Fairy of Magic in North Clock Town after you return her single Stray Fairy (this also gives Link his Magic Power).

COMPENDIUM.name: Great Spin Attack
COMPENDIUM.effect: A longer-charged, far more powerful version of the Spin Attack — double the power and reach, the charge aura advancing past blue.
COMPENDIUM.where: Granted by the Great Fairy in the Southern Swamp after you collect and return all 15 Stray Fairies of Woodfall Temple to her Fairy's Fountain (same in the N64 original and the 3DS remake).

COMPENDIUM.name: Adult Wallet
COMPENDIUM.effect: Raises Link's Rupee capacity from 99 to 200.
COMPENDIUM.where: Deposit a cumulative total of 200 or more Rupees at the Clock Town Bank (West Clock Town); the banker rewards you with the Adult Wallet.

COMPENDIUM.name: Giant's Wallet
COMPENDIUM.effect: Raises Link's Rupee capacity to 500 (needed to afford the All-Night Mask).
COMPENDIUM.where: Clear all 30 Gold Skulltulas from the Oceanside (Swamp) Spider House on the First Day; the man at the entrance then gives you the Giant's Wallet. (In MM3D you can claim it on any day.)

COMPENDIUM.name: Large Quiver
COMPENDIUM.effect: Increases the Hero's Bow's arrow capacity from 30 to 40.
COMPENDIUM.where: Score 40+ points at the Town Shooting Gallery in East Clock Town.

COMPENDIUM.name: Largest Quiver
COMPENDIUM.effect: Increases the Hero's Bow's arrow capacity from 40 to 50.
COMPENDIUM.where: Score a perfect score at the Swamp Shooting Gallery (Southern Swamp).

COMPENDIUM.name: Big Bomb Bag
COMPENDIUM.effect: Increases bomb capacity from 20 to 30.
COMPENDIUM.where: Save the Bomb Shop's old lady from the thief Sakon in North Clock Town around midnight on the First Night (she also rewards the Blast Mask); afterward buy the Big Bomb Bag from the Bomb Shop in West Clock Town for 90 Rupees.

COMPENDIUM.name: Biggest Bomb Bag
COMPENDIUM.effect: Increases bomb capacity from 30 to 40.
COMPENDIUM.where: With the Big Bomb Bag and Goron Mask, talk to the Business Scrub in Goron Village as Goron Link to buy the Biggest Bomb Bag for 200 Rupees.

RUNES.name: Deku Mask
RUNES.from: The Happy Mask Salesman plays the Song of Healing in the Clock Tower after you recover the Ocarina — your Deku-Scrub curse is sealed into a wearable mask.
RUNES.what: Wear it (from the masks menu) to become a Deku Scrub: hop across water on lily pads, shoot bubbles, and launch from Deku Flowers. Take it off to be Hylian again.
RUNES.tip: Transformation masks change WHO you are, not just what you wear. Some doors, switches, and water only respond to the right form.

RUNES.name: Ocarina of Time
RUNES.from: Recovered from the Skull Kid atop the Clock Tower at midnight on the Final Day.
RUNES.what: Your instrument. Songs played on it bend time, warp you, summon Epona, and solve puzzles — the heart of the whole game.
RUNES.tip: Open the ocarina with the song you assigned it to; the notes you press appear on screen as you're taught each melody.

RUNES.name: Song of Time
RUNES.from: Remembered the instant you touch the Ocarina again — Princess Zelda taught it to you before your journey.
RUNES.what: Plays you back to the Dawn of the First Day, resetting the three-day cycle. You KEEP masks, songs, key quest items, bottles, and Heart Containers; you lose rupees, ammo, dungeon keys, and Stray Fairies.
RUNES.tip: Bank rupees in the Clock Town bank before you reset — the banker remembers your balance across cycles. Save your real progress by tying it to masks, songs, and Remains.

RUNES.name: Song of Healing
RUNES.from: Taught by the Happy Mask Salesman in the Clock Tower once you have the Ocarina back.
RUNES.what: Soothes a troubled spirit and seals it into a mask. It lifts your own Deku curse first, then later heals Darmani and Mikau into the Goron and Zora Masks.
RUNES.tip: Whenever someone is grieving or cursed and Tatl hints at it, the Song of Healing is usually the answer — and you walk away with a new mask.

RUNES.name: Hero's Bow
RUNES.from: A chest inside Woodfall Temple, the first dungeon.
RUNES.what: Your main ranged weapon as Hylian Link — shoot eye-switches, distant targets, and (with elemental arrows) freeze or ignite things.
RUNES.tip: You can buy bigger Quivers later. Fire and Ice Arrows come from the temples; Light Arrows are the key to the final fight.

RUNES.name: Bomb Bag
RUNES.from: Won at the Town Shooting Gallery, or bought from the Bomb Shop in West Clock Town (after stopping the robber).
RUNES.what: Lets you carry and throw Bombs to blast cracked walls and boulders and to stun enemies. Bombchu crawl along walls and floors first.
RUNES.tip: Bombs and arrows are consumables — they reset every cycle. Restock cheaply from the Bomb Shop or Bomb Flowers before a dungeon run.

CHAMPIONS.name: Odolwa's Remains
CHAMPIONS.note: Defeat Odolwa, the masked jungle warrior, to free the Giant of the Southern Swamp and earn his Remains.

CHAMPIONS.name: Goht's Remains
CHAMPIONS.note: Defeat Goht, the mechanical bull, to free the Giant of the snowy mountains and earn his Remains.

CHAMPIONS.name: Gyorg's Remains
CHAMPIONS.note: Defeat Gyorg, the giant masked fish, to free the Giant of the ocean and earn his Remains.

CHAMPIONS.name: Twinmold's Remains
CHAMPIONS.note: Defeat Twinmold, the pair of giant sand serpents, to free the Giant of Ikana Canyon and earn his Remains.

ROADMAP.name: The 24 Masks
ROADMAP.note: Twenty regular masks (each from helping someone) plus the four transformation masks. Give the twenty regular masks to the Moon Children to earn the Fierce Deity's Mask.

ROADMAP.name: Pieces of Heart
ROADMAP.note: Fifty-two are hidden across Termina in minigames, trades, and corners — the most of any Zelda game. Every four make a full heart.

ROADMAP.name: Bombers' Notebook
ROADMAP.note: A planner the Bombers gang gives you. It logs every person whose problem you can solve and exactly WHEN over the three days to find them.

ROADMAP.name: Every Ocarina Song
ROADMAP.note: Songs slow time, skip to dawn or night, warp you between owl statues, and free each Giant — collecting them all is how you bend the three-day clock to your will.

TIPS.name: The three-day clock
items: You have three in-game days (about 54 real minutes) before the Moon falls. Play the Song of Time to rewind to the Dawn of the First Day whenever you need more time — or before the Moon lands.
Resetting KEEPS what defines progress: masks, songs, Heart Containers, bottles, and the boss Remains. It WIPES the temporary: rupees, bombs, arrows, dungeon (small) keys, and Stray Fairies.
So finish a temple in one cycle if you can, and bank your rupees at the Clock Town bank first — the banker remembers your balance across resets.

TIPS.name: Wearing a new face
items: The Deku, Goron, and Zora Masks transform Link into that race, each with its own moves (Deku hops on water and flies from flowers; Goron rolls and pounds; Zora swims and throws fin-boomerangs).
Many puzzles only work in the right form — if you're stuck, try a different mask. Regular masks (Bunny Hood, Bremen Mask, Stone Mask…) give smaller perks and solve specific problems.
The Fierce Deity's Mask, won by giving away the twenty regular masks in the Moon, turns Link into a towering swordsman — but only inside boss rooms.

TIPS.name: Bending time
items: The Inverted Song of Time (play the Song of Time backwards) slows time to a crawl — learn it from the Scarecrow and use it to survive a long dungeon in one cycle.
The Song of Double Time jumps straight ahead to the next dawn, noon, or night so you don't have to wait around.
Use the Song of Soaring (Owl statues) to warp across Termina, and the Bombers' Notebook to know exactly when and where each person will be.

COLLECTIBLES.label: Masks
COLLECTIBLES.note: 20 regular + 4 transformation. Give the 20 regular masks to the Moon Children for the Fierce Deity's Mask.

COLLECTIBLES.label: Pieces of Heart
COLLECTIBLES.note: Every 4 make a Heart Container — 13 hearts' worth, the most of any Zelda game.
